goal: A QA checklist is produced and committed covering the headline M1-M3 flows, each row with steps + expected result + a Status/Tester/Date column the operator fills on prod, and a code-evidence column citing the prior verification that already proves the invariant.
why: Handoff QA in this environment is the authored, grounded checklist; the operator owns the live prod execution and sign-off. The auth-gated, key-dependent live flows cannot be run from the build environment, so we deliver the artifact the operator executes against, with code-level evidence pre-attached where a prior phase verification already proved the invariant.
success_criteria: docs/qa-checklist.md is committed and covers all six headline flow groups (auth gate; agent chat + tool trace; confirm/undo; mail send + schedule/cron; OneDrive list+download; mobile at 375px on Emails/Files/Prep), one row per flow, columns Flow | Steps | Expected | Code-evidence | Status | Tester | Date.

Every Flow/Steps/Expected/Code-evidence cell is authored with concrete content; Code-evidence cites a real file:line or archived verification for each invariant already proven at code level. Status/Tester/Date are left blank for the operator (no pre-filled names or dates, no false claim of a live prod run).
The intro states the operator executes the live flows on production and the build environment cannot.
docs/qa-automated-gate.md records the developer-run automated gate (typecheck + tests, both passing) and points to the checklist for the live-prod portion.
npm run typecheck and npm test both exit 0.

tasks.title: Author and commit the headline QA checklist
acceptance_criteria: docs/qa-checklist.md exists with an intro stating the operator runs the live flows on production and the build environment cannot (auth-gated, key-dependent); Status/Tester/Date left blank, no pre-filled names/dates, no claim a live prod run happened.

A Markdown table per six flow groups (auth gate; agent chat + tool trace; confirm/undo; mail send + schedule/cron; OneDrive list+download; mobile 375px on Emails/Files/Prep), columns Flow | Steps | Expected | Code-evidence | Status | Tester | Date.

Code-evidence cells cite real file:line / archived verifications: src/proxy.ts, executeConfirmedAction.ts, onedrive/download, scheduled/run, globals.css:331, and the archived phase-3-verification reports.
No TODO/FIXME/TBD placeholders in authored columns; a How-to-use closing section instructs the operator to mark Pass/Fail and resolve any Fail before close.
tasks.action: Create docs/qa-checklist.md with a caveat intro, one heading + table per flow group (header Flow | Steps | Expected | Code-evidence | Status | Tester | Date), concrete operator steps + expected results, file:line / archived-verification citations in the Code-evidence cell, blank Status/Tester/Date cells, and a How-to-use closing section. Do not pre-fill testers/dates or assert a prod run occurred.

verification.pattern: \| Flow \| Steps \| Expected \| Code-evidence \| Status \| Tester \| Date \|

tasks.title: Record the committed automated QA gate (typecheck + tests)
acceptance_criteria: docs/qa-automated-gate.md exists and documents the two developer-run commands (npm run typecheck, npm test) with expected exit 0, copy-pasteable.
Records the run observed at authoring time: tsc --noEmit exit 0; vitest 12 files / 59 tests passed.
States the boundary: these gates prove compile + seam tests, not the live auth-gated/key-dependent prod flows, and cross-references docs/qa-checklist.md as the operator's surface.
tasks.action: Create docs/qa-automated-gate.md with a Gate|Command|Expected table (typecheck via npm run typecheck; test suite via npm test), a Last-run line (tsc exit 0; vitest 12 files / 59 tests passed), and a boundary paragraph pointing to docs/qa-checklist.md for the live-prod portion.
